faqs.question: Why are crickets so common in Fordham buildings?
faqs.answer: Fordham's combination of older construction, ground-level retail spaces, and proximity to parks and green areas creates perfect conditions for crickets. These insects are attracted to moisture, warmth, and the numerous entry points found in the neighborhood's pre-war buildings and commercial structures along Fordham Road.

We provide follow-up services to ensure all crickets and their eggs are eradicated.
